_doc: CUDA-side template for the Metal<->CUDA MTP spec-decode bit-exact cross-check. charlie fills generated_ids from the CUDA `mtp-specdecode` inferlet (bin/pie/tests/cuda_mtp_specdecode_ab.rs) on the SAME prompt, then: `python3 crosscheck.py --mtp-cuda mtp_cuda_results.json`. Schema = mtp_specdecode_crosscheck_v1.
_BLOCKED: (e) bit-exact is GATED on the CUDA absolute-decode fix. charlie's actual 4090 A/B run (2026-07-07) came back committed[6:] = ALL ZEROS on BOTH the device fast-path AND the host-round-trip — a known GDN/FLA commit-advance fold bug (rs_cache T1 xfail, bravo's fix pending). The accept LOGIC is value-correct (mean_accept ~0.67/0.78, Δ≈0) but the absolute token VALUES are zeroed. So CUDA's absolute decode is currently BROKEN; Metal's absolutes ([279,5983,40289,13,198]='the lazy dog', committed==greedy bit-exact) are the CORRECT oracle. crosscheck.py auto-detects the all-zero tail and reports CUDA-BUG (not a Metal divergence). Re-run this cross-check once bravo's rs_cache commit-advance fix lands and CUDA's absolute decode produces real tokens.
_align: PROMPT (charlie, dev 2535e29d): 'The quick brown fox jumps over' => prompt_ids [760,3841,13477,37550,33075,888] (n=6, NO BOS). Metal committed==greedy on this prompt: generated_ids [279,5983,40289,13,198,760,3841,13477,37550,33075,888,279,5983,40289,13,198] ('the lazy dog.' then greedy-repeats the prompt). k=4, MAX_TOKENS=16, greedy verify.
_note: committed==greedy on BOTH backends, so k (4 vs Metal's 1) does NOT change generated_ids — only fires/acceptance. So generated_ids MUST be bit-identical Metal==CUDA iff the GDN backbones agree numerically. charlie's committed INCLUDES the 6 prompt ids at the front; the harness compares generated_ids (the post-prompt tail), so paste committed[6:] here as generated_ids (or the full committed and set prompt_ids to the 6).
generated_ids: FILL: CUDA committed generated tail (committed[6:]), length up to MAX_TOKENS=16
